mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows, or forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) across 2000-2005 data.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger immediate equity sell-offs over the following week.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day forward returns occur in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to separate bull and bear market weeks with an accuracy above 52%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving corporate earnings revisions and positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 60-day returns in over 60% of occurrences, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.05) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect strengthening manufacturing output that supports equity valuations, but this mechanism only operates during confirmed economic expansions.
failure_condition: The signal generates negative forward returns during expansion phases, or the predictive power vanishes when filtered for NBER-defined expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth exceeding trend levels signals robust economic activity that immediately boosts market sentiment and generates positive weekly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain flat or negative following positive GDP surprises, or the correlation between GDP growth and weekly returns drops below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly CPI inflation surpassing 0.4 percent triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity multiples and producing negative quarterly returns.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally following high inflation prints, or the threshold fails to predict negative returns with a win rate above 50% over 60-day horizons.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and declines in the unemployment rate confirm labor market tightening that supports consumer spending and positive monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields negative 20-day returns in more than 58% of cases, or the interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp accelerations in industrial production drive short-term equity rallies, but this mechanism reverses during late-cycle phases where capacity constraints dominate.
failure_condition: The signal fails to generate positive 5-day returns during late-cycle periods, or predictive accuracy drops below 48% when applied to post-2003 data.
